message:
feat(conformance): respect HarnessConfig.strict_mode when selecting Runtime

Previously, all conformance harness functions (run_fixture, run_live_redis_diff,
run_protocol_fixture, run_live_redis_protocol_diff, run_replay_fixture)
unconditionally created Runtime::default_strict(), ignoring the strict_mode
flag on HarnessConfig. This meant hardened-mode conformance testing was
impossible through the standard harness path.

Extract a runtime_for_harness_config() helper that returns
Runtime::default_strict() when strict_mode is true and
Runtime::default_hardened() when false. Replace all five call sites that
previously hard-coded Runtime::default_strict().

This enables differential testing between strict and hardened Runtime modes,
which is important for validating that threat-severity expectations diverge
correctly in hardened mode (protocol_negative fixture surfaces severity
mismatches rather than silently passing).

New tests:
- runtime_for_harness_config_selects_mode: directly validates that the
  helper returns the correct Runtime variant by checking evidence event
  mode tags (Mode::Strict vs Mode::Hardened) after executing a NOAUTH
  scenario.
- conformance_errors_fixture_passes_in_hardened_mode: runs core_errors.json
  with strict_mode=false.
- conformance_protocol_fixture_exposes_expected_hardened_threat_drift:
  verifies protocol_negative.json produces expected severity-mismatch
  failures in hardened mode.
- run_protocol_fixture_persists_mode_selected_by_harness_config: end-to-end
  test that runs protocol_negative.json in both modes, asserts structured
  log events carry the correct LogMode (Strict vs Hardened), and validates
  pass/fail expectations per mode.
- conformance_replay_fixture_passes_in_hardened_mode: runs persist_replay.json
  with strict_mode=false.

Import additions: runtime_for_harness_config (test visibility), LogMode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
